info.title: Ziwo API
info.description: Ziwo Cloud Contact Center is an app with readymade CRM plugins to connect real or virtual agents with phone numbers in any country.

Optionally filter by from date in YYYY-DD-MM format.
The from time filter in HH:MM:SS format, defaults to 00:00:00 when fromDate is only given.
Optionally filter by to date in YYYY-DD-MM format.
The to time filter in HH:MM:SS format, defaults to 23:59:59 when toDate is only given.
Filter by the type of the log, Possible options are startCall and endCall.
Filter by the status of the request. Possible options are success and failed.
Filter/Search by callID.

Filter by time taken to by the request in milliseconds. If this filter is given once, logs will be filtered by time taken > given value. If given twice (Ex: totalTime=23&totalTime=50) the logs will be filtered by time taken between first value and second value.
Order by the field given. Currently suports only createdAt field. Prefix with ~ for desceding order (default).
The number of records to be fetched.
The number of rows to be skipped.
[Deprecated, please use limit and skip instead] This is use to paginate the result set e.g. setting fetchStart=30 means it will output the result set from the 30th log.
[Deprecated, please use limit and skip instead] This is the number of records to fetch per page for pagination. e.g. setting fetchStop=100 will fetch 100 logs from the starting point as fetchStart
The timezone to consider. If not given, the default instance time will be considered. Here is the list of available timezones

post.summary: IVR - Make a call (Click to IVR)

description.content: Customize number to be displayed on the receiver's phone. (This value has be to within the range Stark/Ziwo can accept).
(Optional) The maximum number of seconds to wait for an answer state from a the external number. >=v1.4.2-beta.

The authentication key that that was provided while sending the SMS.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
The Auth module should be your first step to access the APIs and all the other endpoints in it.

Types of Authentications.

Access token

You can use this module to generate a long-term and short-term access tokens which should be passed in the header of every request which requires authentication. The access_token is generally valid for 24 hours by default but it can also be generated for longer period. Instr...

tags.name: Numbers
tags.description:
The Numbers (Call Center Numbers) module is for handling incoming calls which land into the platform and what to do with the call. When the telecom provider is routing the calls to your platform IP, you add the number to this module and configure the handling.

The routing of the incoming call can be handled in the below possible ways:

  • Send the call to a queue. (linkType: queue, linkData: name of the queue)
  • Send the call to an IVR. (linkTy...

tags.name: Call Masking / Extension Maps
tags.description:
Call Masking / Extension maps allow admins to create extensions with target routes. When a caller is making a call, if the called number is linked with an extension, it will allow/ask user to enter an extension and system will auto route the caller to the taregt based on the extered extension.

For example, let's say we need to maitain a map of travellers and assign a unique numeric extension to each of the traveller and upon the driver calling Ziwo, system can auto route call to the ta...

tags.name: WhatsApp Numbers
tags.description:
The WhatsApp Numbers module is for handling WhatsApp messages.

When the customer messages to one of this WhatsApp number, based on the routing configuration, the messages will be routed to the agent and upon the agent replies, the replied messages will be routed through the same WhatsApp Number to the customer WhatsApp.

{
  "id": "3eb7535d-1547-4169-b84e-d39c546ffbc0",
  "isPrimary": tr...

tags.name: Agents
tags.description:
The Agents module handles all the agent accounts which will be used for logging-in to the platform through one of the IP, Soft or Web phones. Every agent account will have few attributes such as username, ccLogin and ccPassword.

  • The username and password is used for agent to login to the web console to view the profile, call evaluations, statistics and performance of that agent.
  • The ccLog...

tags.name: Queues
tags.description: The Queues are like buckets for the support requests (calls, tickets, sms etc). All similar support requests are grouped togther in to the queues. The agents are linked to queues based on the skills assigned to the agents. When a support request lands in a queue, the support request is assigned to the agent which are assigned to the queue through skill. If there are no free agents, the support request is marked as "waiting in queue". Once the agent is free, ...

tags.name: Skills
tags.description:
Skills module is a bridge between agents and queues. It allows to configure which agent has an ability to receive a support reques from which queue based on priority.

An agent can be linked to multiple queues (thus, can have multiple skills)
A queue can be linked to multiple agents (thus, a queue can consist of multiple agents)
Skill priority is used to decide which call goes to which agent when there are more then 1 agent available for the answering the support request.

...

tags.name: Positions
tags.description:
Any call which needs to be handled by the users (agents/admins) needs to have a connection to platform. When Ziwo offers a call to/from the agent, this information will be exchanged over this network connection. To make this connection, user needs to have position accounts. The position account is a mainly a unique username and password.

Types of positions:

  • normal: Created by admin module. This is have a username and password configured by the admin.
  • virtual: Syst...

tags.name: Gateways
tags.description:
Gateways handle all the sip gateways through which the calls come in (incoming) and go out (outgoing) of the platform. Every gateway will have few attributes such as host and possibly username and password with which a gateway can be added.

The gateways can be added on the basis of only host i.e. non-registered gateway - which means it will not maintain a live connection with the endpoint and requests initiated from the h...

tags.name: Roles
tags.description:
The roles module handles all the permissions for user accounts. Role can be a system defined static role or user can create roles with various permission sets. User will be allowed to access/manage a module only if he/she has been assigned with a role which has nesessary permissions for the same module.

There are 2 types of permissions available. One is for statis modules. One is for the resources within the modules. For example one role can have access to the full module live, queues,...

tags.name: Settings
tags.description:
This module handles the settings/configuration of the App. Some example settings are: SLA percentage, Phantom Calls Seconds etc. Each setting entry will have a name, data, category and the sub category.

There are predefined settings with general category and default subCategory, which will be used by the App. Update Settings API can be used to update these values. Currently, app doesn't have/use any other categories/sub categories.

tags.name: Static
tags.description: This module static has set of APIs which gives static contansts, settings information which can be used in other APIs. For example, valid queue strategy types which has to be used while creating/updating the queues.

tags.name: Smart/Dynamic Routers
tags.description:
The Dynamic Routers (Blacklist) module is for dynamically routing incoming calls which land to the platform. When an incoming call matches a dynamic routing entry, a different set of actions / steps are executed (than regular steps) for that call as defined by that dynamic router.

The dynamic routing can be configured in the below possible ways:

  • Single number
  • Range of numbers
  • Country code

The dynamic routed incoming calls can be...

tags.name: Call Trackers
tags.description: The Call Trackers module can be used for dynamic handling the incoming calls by making a webservice request to external service. (Specs are mentioned below). This service can then reply saying what to do with the call. To implement these services from your end, you can look into example start and end request here: https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/1450071/RWToNwzB?version...

tags.name: Call Tracker Examples
tags.description: This section is just for demonstration on how call tracker urls have to be implemented.

tags.name: Audit
tags.description:
When system goes through various activities, this module records all these activities made by the users/agents/system. This module provides following APIs to get these activities:

  • Get Audit Logs (With various filters)
  • Get Audit Log (detailed information, by id)

tags.name: Agent Groups
tags.description: The Agent Groups module handles the set of agent accounts. It's much easier to manage groups than to manage individuals in large call centers with multiple teams etc.

tags.name: Storage
tags.description:
This storage module contains set of file objects which can be used in the app like audio file to be played when music is on hold, or a profile image file etc.

  • There are certain categories under which storage objects can be created like ivr, audio, image etc. For the exact list, refer to Static section.

  • There are also limits to these storage file sizes. For the exact limits of each caregory, refer to Static section.

tags.name: Zoho
tags.description: These APIs are to connect/integrate Zoho CRM with Ziwo dialer.

tags.name: Webhook
tags.description:
The Webhook is a module which will post the resource creation/updation details to the external web services. Currently supported resource is the cdrs.

Example: Whenever a cdr (call detail record) is created/updated, the details will be posted to the configured external URL. This configuration will done at the cdr webhook settings.

tags.name: Statuses
tags.description:
Manage the statuses the agents can use.

The system will have a few statuses created for you by default, but incase if you would like to add, or disable, you can use these APIs to manage them.

The status contains the following fields.

  • name: a unique name for status.
  • type: A status can be default or custom. The default statuses are immutable.
  • enabl...

tags.name: Template Attachments
tags.description: These APIs support uploading and downloading the attachments which can be used while creating the multi-media templates.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description:
Manage the tags.

Tags are of three types: Call Tags, Conversation Tags and the Customer Tags

The call tags are to attach for the call entries / cdrs.

The conversation tags are to attach to the conversations.

The customer tags are to attach to the customer (contact) entries.

The tag contains the following fields.

  • name: a unique name for the tag.
  • enabled: (true/false) A tag can be in enable...

tags.name: CTI
tags.description:
The CTI module is one of the available apps which is a part of the integrations suite.

The module can be used to manipulate the state of the the platform (e.g. auto-login of agent, changing the status of an agent, initiating a call etc.)

To access the routes for CTI, an api_key is the required token instead of access_token. It can be passed in various ways in the request as follows:

  • Requ...

tags.name: Preferences
tags.description:
User Preferences module can be used to store any preferences of a perticular user. For ex: last visited page, last dialed number etc. It is also possible to create shared preferences.

There is no specific structure that has to be followed while storing preferences. Any datatype (string, integer, array or a key value pair can be stored). Ex: while storing the last dialed number, it can just be a string. while storing the dashboard widget info, it can be a nested array/dict.

tags.name: [Removed] CRM
tags.description:
These APIs are disabled and not be used. Please consider using the new CRM APIs.

CRM - Customers

The CRM - Customers (CDB) module is a typical CRM system for managing customers and its related information. A single customer can consist the following

  • One profile
  • Multiple addresses
  • Multiple numbers
  • Multiple social accounts

The general idea behind the different entit...

tags.name: [Removed] Integrations - CRM
tags.description:
These APIs are disabled and not be used. Please consider using the new CRM APIs.

The CRM - Customers (CDB) module is a typical CRM system for managing customers and its related information. A single customer can consist the following

  • One profile
  • Multiple addresses
  • Multiple numbers
  • Multiple social accounts

The general idea behind the different entities is that multiple entities can be linked wi...

tags.name: Call history (CDR)
tags.description:
Fields

CDR Response now contain a new integer field flags which represent a combination of bitwise flags and currently contain following flags:
FLAG_NON_WORKING_HOURS = 1 << 0 // 1
FLAG_IS_IVR_CALL = 1 << 1 // 2
FLAG_LOST_IN_IVR = 1 << ...

tags.name: Live
tags.description:
The Live module publishes live state of the platform. Any changes to agents, positions, queues and stats is published from the server to the connected socket clients. The clients can connect to the live module via web socket and listen on events which are related to state changes for the above mentioned objects

The web socket connection needs to be authenticated, by the same access_token (generated through login request) used for all other requests. Once the socket conncetion is authen...

tags.name: Call Whispering
tags.description:
This feature initiates a eavesdrop/whisper call which let's user/admin to listen to an agent's live conversation with the customers. This call can also be updated to speak to agent/both. When the specified agent is not on a call, this eavesdrop call plays the hold music.

This initiated eavesdrop call rings the current user(admin) at his/her default position (unless position name is explicitly specifi...

tags.name: Statistics
tags.description: The statistic module proivides APIs to compute statistics/reports of various datasources along with various possible filters. Please check individual APIs for more details about each APIs.

tags.name: Tools
tags.description: This module proivdes utility APIs like convert JSON to CSV etc.

tags.name: Support
tags.description: This endpoint allows to reach aswat various supports.

tags.name: Conversations
tags.description:
A conversation in Ziwo refers to a case or a query for which customer contacts the call center (or vice versa).

Following are the conversation types available:

The type of conversation cannot not be used when the mutichannel conversation mode is enabled. As multi channel conversations can have all t...

tags.name: WhatsApp
tags.description: This section describes the APIs available for the Admin users to send WhatsApp messages. These APIs are very similar to what's available for the Agent users.

tags.name: [Deprecated] Jorel Client
tags.description:
What is Jorel ?

Jorel is the third party dialpad available for all customers, and comes as an external script to include.
It simply represent the dialpad of the Agent Client.

Definition: Contact Center Name

If your Ziwo front url is company-name.aswat.co then your contact center name is company-name.
This definition is useful in several parts of this Jorel documentation.

tags.name: How to use Jorel ?
tags.description:
To use Jorel, you just need to add the below script in your web application.
Simply set the variable contactCenterName in the first line of the script with the name of your contact center.
(See contact center name definition above)

If you want a non call center specific integration, you can leave this variable empty.
Then user will be asked contact center name during each login step.

tags.name: WebCallback Client
tags.description:
The WebCallback integration is a button you can add to your website to allow customers to be called back. This invokes the WebCallback API.

The customers who want to be called back just have to click on the button and enter their phone number, then Ziwo will generate a call to this customer, linking them to the contact center number you chose.

tags.name: Internal
tags.description: These APIs are internally used to communicate with different services and are not to be used directly by end customers.

tags.name: FS - Core
tags.description:
This is an internal module which handles requests from underneath telephony system FreeSWITCH. These supply details like queues, agents, skills etc from the app to the telephony system.

These APIs request and response structures are not same as other module APIs as these are cusomized as per the needs of underneath telephony system.

These APIs are not meant (not available) for end users.

INTERNAL KEY...

tags.name: FS - CDR
tags.description:
This is an internal module which handles CDR/Call requests from underneath telephony system FreeSWITCH.

These APIs request and response structure are not same as other module APIs as these are cusomized as per the needs of underneath telephony system.

These APIs are not meant (not available) for end users.

INTERNAL KEY_ONLY

tags.name: Error Codes
tags.description:
This section describes various error codes that server may return and what do they mean. Using this code, the client can display useful information or resolution flow according to client's need.
Agent Creation

1: The license information is not available, please c...
